English: Letter A. Pronunciation: 'ah' (like 'father') or short 'a' (like 'cap'). Examples: 'appel' (apple)
Letter D. Pronunciation: 'day' (sounds like 't' at the end of a word). Examples: 'dak' (roof)
Letter E. Pronunciation: 'ay' (like 'say') or short 'eh' (like 'pet'). Examples: 'ezel' (donkey)
Letter H. Pronunciation: 'ha' (breathy as in English). Examples: 'huis' (house)
Letter I. Pronunciation: 'ee' (long) or short 'i' (like 'sit'). Examples: 'ik' (I / me)
Letter J. Pronunciation: 'je' (sounds like 'y' in 'yellow'). Examples: 'jas' (coat)
Letter O. Pronunciation: 'oh' (like 'go') or short 'o' (like 'pot'). Examples: 'oog' (eye)
Letter Q. Pronunciation: 'ku' (rare; sounds like 'k'). Examples: 'quiz' (quiz)
Letter U. Pronunciation: 'u' (French 'u' / German 'ü') or short 'uh' (like 'sir'). Examples: 'uur' (hour)
Letter Y. Pronunciation: 'griekse ij' (called Greek Y). Examples: 'yoghurt' (yogurt)
Long AA Vowel. Pronunciation: 'ah' (long and open). Examples: 'maan' (moon)
Long EE Vowel. Pronunciation: 'ay' (long and tense). Examples: 'been' (leg)
Long OO Vowel. Pronunciation: 'oh' (long and rounded). Examples: 'boot' (boat)
Long UU Vowel. Pronunciation: 'uu' (rounded French 'u'). Examples: 'muur' (wall)
Vowel digraph EU. Pronunciation: 'eu' (French 'eu' / German 'ö'). Examples: 'neus' (nose)
Diphthong UI. Pronunciation: rounded front vowel (French 'oeil'). Examples: 'huis' (house)
